qa_pairs.question: What are the key criteria and their corresponding weights used to evaluate GPU models for Reference GPU Units (RGUs)?
qa_pairs.answer: GPU models are evaluated for Reference GPU Units (RGUs) based on three main criteria: FP32 score (for dense matrices on regular GPU cores) which accounts for 40% of the weight, FP16 score (for dense matrices on Tensor cores) also weighted at 40%, and GPU memory score which makes up the remaining 20%. The NVidia A100-40gb GPU is used as the reference model, assigned an RGU value of 4.0.

qa_pairs.question: Could you list the Reference GPU Unit (RGU) scores for various whole GPU models available for allocation?
qa_pairs.answer: The NVidia A100-40gb GPU is the reference model with a combined RGU score of 4.0. Other whole GPU models and their combined RGU scores are: H100-80gb (12.2 RGU), A100-80gb (4.8 RGU), V100-32gb (2.6 RGU), V100-16gb (2.2 RGU), T4-16gb (1.3 RGU), P100-16gb (1.1 RGU), and P100-12gb (1.0 RGU).

qa_pairs.question: What are Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) instances, and how are their Reference GPU Unit (RGU) values determined for resource allocation?
qa_pairs.answer: Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology allows scheduling a fraction of a GPU, referred to as a GPU instance or MIG instance, enabling multiple jobs or users to run simultaneously on the same GPU. For allocation purposes, the RGU values for these GPU instances are estimated by considering the whole-GPU performance numbers and the specific fraction of the GPU that the instance represents. For example, an A100-1g.5gb instance is assigned an RGU value of 0.6.
